output: AI stands for Artificial Intelligence which enables machines to simulate human intelligence.
Machine Learning is a subset of AI where systems learn patterns from data.
Deep Learning uses neural networks with multiple layers to learn complex patterns.
Fine-tuning is the process of training a pretrained model on task-specific data.
LoRA is a parameter-efficient fine-tuning technique that trains low-rank matrices instead of full model weights.
QLoRA combines quantization and LoRA to reduce GPU memory usage during fine-tuning.
NLP stands for Natural Language Processing and focuses on understanding human language.
Transformers are deep learning architectures that use self-attention mechanisms for sequence processing.
Supervised learning trains models using labeled input-output data.
Overfitting occurs when a model memorizes training data and performs poorly on new data.
